$comment: D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN (TF arc C10, C89 6.7.1): block-scope `extern` declarations inside a function body — a FUNCTION prototype (`extern int helper(int);`) AND an OBJECT reference (`extern int g_base;`), both defined in the sibling TU def.c. The whole-program LK11 merge binds the block externs to def.c's definitions (function import stripped + call rewired direct; object load reaches the global). helper(g_base) = helper(40) = 42. This is the real SQLite src/test*.c shape (6/44 TUs block-declare externs of symbols defined in other TUs). RED-ON-DISABLE (grammar): drop `externDecl` from the `statement` alt in c-subset.lang.json -> P0009 'got extern'. RED-ON-DISABLE (lowering): drop the k=='ExternDecl' arm in cst_to_hir.cpp lowerStmt -> 'statement maps to unsupported HIR kind ExternDecl'. The cross-CU shape mirrors bare_proto_crosscu / extern_incomplete_array_crosscu (pe + elf run targets; macho 2-TU stays off this manifest — underscore-mangled cross-CU extern matching is untested there).
